wifi_version.description:
Wi-Fi operation mode:
`1` = Wi-Fi 1 (802.11b) (Only 2.4 GHz),
`2` = Wi-Fi 2 (IEEE 802.11a) (Only 5 GHz),
`3` = Wi-Fi 3 (IEEE 802.11g) (only 2.4 GHz),
`4` = Wi-Fi 4 (IEEE 802.11n) (2.4/5 GHz),
`5` = Wi-Fi 5 (IEEE 802.11ac) (only 5 GHz),
`6` = Wi-Fi 6/6E (IEEE 802.11ax) (2.4/5 GHz for Wi-Fi 6, 2.4/5/6 GHz for Wi-Fi 6E),
`7` = Wi-Fi 7 (IEEE 802.11be) (2.4/5/6 GHz)

band.description: Wi-Fi Operating frequency: `2.4` OR `5`. The default should be fine. 6 GHz is NOT supported!

country_code.description: Wi-Fi Country code (ISO/IEC 3166-1). Used to set regulatory domain. Set as needed to indicate country in which device is operating. This can limit available channels and transmit power.

wpa_passphrase.description: Wi-Fi password used as the WPA pre-shared key (WPA-PSK)

ble_password.description: BLE password to communicate with companion app, please set it on app too

logfile.description: Log file path, by default it is saved to `/var/log` in `tmpfs`/memory. If you want, you can change it e.g. to `/data/aa-proxy-rs.log` and it will be saved to SD card (appended, not replaced).

crash_handler_enabled.description: Enable Rust panic report files. Normal runtime cost is negligible; stacktrace capture and file write happen only when a panic occurs.

crash_dir.description: Directory where Rust panic reports are written with timestamped filenames. Default: `/data/aa-proxy-rs/crashes`.

stats_interval.description: Interval of showing data transfer statistics in the log (0 = disabled) [seconds]

timeout_secs.description: Data transfer timeout [seconds], after this idle time the session will be reconnected

legacy.description: Enable legacy USB mode (some HeadUnits/cars needs this enabled for compatibility)

quick_reconnect.description: EXPERIMENTAL: Maintain persistent Bluetooth connection to enable faster reconnection with MD

wired.description:
Enable wired USB connection to phone (VID:PID should be specified, zero is wildcard and can be used for single or both fields)
you can obtain it e.g. using `lsusb` after connecting phone,
and then use e.g. "18d1:0" which will handle specified phone vendor ID (Google Pixel in this example)

udc.description: UDC Controller name (used in special configurations)

startup_delay.description:
Enables delaying the launch of `aa-proxy-rs` by a specified number of seconds (range: 0–254).
Default value: 0.
Intended for specific scenarios (e.g., non-standard head units)

debug.description: Enable global debug logging. This also enables many non-packet debug messages.

pkt_debug.description: Enable packet debug output independently from global debug logging. When enabled, pkt_debug lines are emitted at INFO level, so debug can stay disabled to avoid unrelated debug noise.

hexdump_level.description: Hex dump level [possible values: Disabled, DecryptedInput, RawInput, DecryptedOutput, RawOutput, All]

disable_console_debug.description: Disable debug level on console, save it only to logfile (helpful for `hexdump-level` option)

pkt_debug_filter_enabled.description: Enable packet debug filtering. This narrows pkt_debug output on top of hexdump_level.

pkt_debug_filter_channels.description: Comma-separated numeric channel IDs to include. Empty = all. Examples: 0x00,0x08,8.

pkt_debug_filter_exclude_channels.description: Comma-separated numeric channel IDs to exclude. Examples: 0x01,1.

pkt_debug_filter_message_ids.description: Comma-separated numeric message IDs to include. Empty = all. Examples: 0x0006,6.

pkt_debug_filter_exclude_message_ids.description: Comma-separated numeric message IDs to exclude.

pkt_debug_filter_pretty_proto.description: When packet debug filtering is enabled, also print decoded protobuf text for known control messages.

pkt_debug_filter_max_payload_bytes.description: When packet debug filtering is enabled, truncate payload dumps to this many bytes. 0 disables truncation.

enable_btle.description: Enable BLE/GATT server for companion app

btalias.description:
Bluetooth device name/alias (how your bluetooth device is visible)
by default it is `aa-proxy-CPUSERIAL`
for dongle mode you need to set it to: `AndroidAuto-xxxxx`

connect.description:
Auto-connect to phone and initiate connection
empty string/no value: don't auto-connect,
zeros: iterate over all previously connected devices,
may be set to a comma seperated list of phone BT MAC addresses for connecting to a specific list of phones, wildcard may not be used with list

bt_timeout_secs.description: Bluetooth handshake timeout [seconds], this is for rare cases when the phone stops responding, to prevent the bluetooth handshake deadlock with inifite waiting and not restarting connection again

bt_connect_timeout_secs.description: Maximum seconds to wait for a single Bluetooth connect_profile() attempt per device [seconds]. When multiple devices are listed in `connect`, each gets one attempt per round before any device is retried. Lower values skip to the next device faster when a phone is absent. Default: 10

iface.description: WLAN / Wi-Fi Hotspot interface (for obtaining IP and BSSID)

change_usb_order.description: Connect to HU USB before Bluetooth handshake

bt_poweroff.description: Powers off the Bluetooth adapter after the handshake. Intended for use only in specific configurations

external_antenna.description: Only for Radxa: enable if you want to use (and have connected) an external antenna

bt_sco.description: Enable the experimental Bluetooth SCO/eSCO call-audio bridge/listener.

bt_sco_keep_bluetooth_alive.description: When bt_sco is enabled, keep the Android Auto Bluetooth profile/RFCOMM connection alive after Wi-Fi bootstrap so the phone continues routing call audio to aa-proxy-rs.

bt_sco_media_bridge.description: Experimental: bridge Bluetooth SCO call downlink into a selected Android Auto PCM sink. Requires MITM and bt_sco listener.

bt_sco_media_bridge_audio_type.description: Preferred AA PCM sink for SCO downlink.

bt_sco_media_bridge_gain_percent.description: SCO downlink output gain percent after conversion. 100 means no gain. Start with 300 if call audio is quiet.

bt_sco_media_bridge_limiter.description: Limiter applied after gain: off preserves the proven path except unavoidable i16 saturation, hard clips peaks earlier, soft compresses peaks more gently.

bt_sco_media_bridge_resampler.description: SCO 8 kHz to AA 48 kHz resampler: repeat preserves the proven path, linear smooths the 6x upsampling and may reduce roughness/crackle.

bt_sco_media_bridge_ring_capacity.description: Maximum converted AA PCM chunks buffered for SCO media bridge. 128 is a safe default; lower values reduce latency.

bt_sco_media_bridge_start_existing.description: When SCO connects, send MEDIA_MESSAGE_START on the selected existing/configured PCM sink before piggyback DATA. Does not send CHANNEL_OPEN or SETUP.

bt_sco_media_bridge_start_on_first_audio.description: Delay MEDIA_MESSAGE_START/DATA until the SCO downlink contains non-silent audio. Useful for diagnosing phone call routes where SCO opens but initially carries silence.

bt_sco_media_bridge_audio_peak_threshold.description: Peak threshold for start_on_first_audio. Converted 16-bit PCM frames below this value are treated as silence. 64 is a conservative starting point.

bt_sco_media_bridge_start_timeout_ms.description: Fallback timeout for start_on_first_audio. If no non-silent SCO downlink is seen within this time, START/DATA begins anyway.

bt_sco_media_bridge_stop_existing_on_dis.description: When SCO disconnects, send MEDIA_MESSAGE_STOP on the selected existing PCM sink. Useful to reset DHU/HU audio renderer state between calls.

bt_sco_media_bridge_fixed_cadence.description: Pace outgoing AA DATA packets at a fixed cadence instead of sending immediately when converted SCO chunks arrive. Disabled by default to preserve the proven behavior.

bt_sco_media_bridge_cadence_ms.description: DATA packet cadence in milliseconds when fixed cadence is enabled. 22 ms matches the current 6-SCO-packet chunk timing closely.

bt_sco_media_bridge_jitter_buffer_ms.description: Minimum converted-audio buffer before the first fixed-cadence DATA packet. 60 ms is a conservative starting point.

bt_sco_mic_bridge.description: Experimental: bridge Android Auto HU microphone/source PCM into Bluetooth SCO call uplink. Requires MITM and bt_sco listener.

bt_sco_mic_request.description: Send MEDIA_MESSAGE_MICROPHONE_REQUEST open/close when SCO connects/disconnects. Disable for passive mic logging only.

bt_sco_mic_uplink_ring_capacity.description: Maximum 60-byte SCO uplink packets buffered for microphone bridge. 256 is a safe default.

bt_sco_mic_echo_control.description: Microphone echo handling for SCO uplink. off preserves current behavior; ducking lowers mic while downlink audio is active.

bt_sco_mic_gain_percent.description: Microphone uplink gain percent after echo processing. 100 means no gain.

bt_sco_mic_duck_threshold.description: Downlink peak threshold that marks far-end audio as active for ducking.

bt_sco_mic_duck_percent.description: Mic gain percent while far-end/downlink audio is active. Lower values reduce echo more but hurt full-duplex speech.

bt_sco_mic_duck_hold_ms.description: How long to keep ducking after the last active downlink frame.

mitm.description: Enable MITM mode. This must be enabled for all options below to take effect.

dpi.description:
Force DPI
0 = do not change DPI
If you are unsure what value to use, start experimenting with e.g. 130. Logs are helpful, as they show both the original HU value and the new one.

sdr_ui_override_enabled.description: Enable per-vehicle SDR UI config overrides for `content_insets`, `stable_content_insets`, and `margins` in media sink video configs.

sdr_ui_override_autocreate_profiles.description: Automatically create disabled SDR UI override profiles from the first observed ServiceDiscoveryResponse for each vehicle.

sdr_ui_override_file.description: TOML file used to store per-vehicle and optional per-phone SDR UI overrides. Default: `/data/aa-proxy-rs/sdr-ui-overrides.toml`.

audio_max_unacked.description:
Override the `max_unacked` setting for audio channels. This may improve audio performance on some head units, but may worsen it on others. Adjust this value experimentally only if you experience audio stuttering.
0 = leave unchanged.

add_vendor_channel.description: Add vendor channel for android companion app

remove_tap_restriction.description: Remove tap restrictions. This affects situations where tapping too frequently while driving triggers a temporary lockout warning. Might not work, depending on phone and Android version.

video_in_motion.description: Enable video playback while driving. Intended for passengers only - do not watch video while driving. Might not work, depending on phone and Android version. In some cases, this also requires `developer_mode` to be enabled below.

disable_media_sink.description: Disable the media sink. This prevents regular audio from being routed to the head unit (it stays on the phone), allowing, for example, a passenger to watch YouTube locally on the phone with audio.

disable_tts_sink.description: Disable the TTS sink. Similar to the option above, but navigation voice guidance is not routed to the head unit and remains on the phone.

developer_mode.description: Enable developer mode. This option emulates a Google Head Unit, allowing installation and use of applications that are normally unavailable. Commonly used by developers when testing or developing new applications.

remove_bluetooth.description: Remove the Bluetooth service from the `service discovery response`. This option may be helpful for head units with integrated wireless Android Auto.

remove_wifi.description: Remove the Wi-Fi service from the `service discovery response`. This option may be helpful for head units with integrated wireless Android Auto.

inject_display_types.description: Add video display services to the service discovery response. Select one or more display types. Leave empty to disable. Useful when forcing additional display channels for capture. Requires mitm = true.

inject_add_input_sources.description: When enabled, also inject matching `input_source_service` entries for configured display IDs. Disabled by default because HU-specific keycode sets may differ.

inject_cluster_display_id.description: Display ID to use for injected cluster display services.

inject_cluster_width_margin.description: Cluster video width margin for injected display service.

inject_cluster_height_margin.description: Cluster video height margin for injected display service.

inject_cluster_density.description: Cluster display density (DPI) for injected display service.

inject_cluster_viewing_distance.description: Cluster viewing distance for injected display service.

inject_cluster_codec_resolution.description: Cluster video codec resolution enum for injected display service.

inject_cluster_touch_width.description: Cluster input touchscreen width when `inject_add_input_sources` is enabled.

inject_cluster_touch_height.description: Cluster input touchscreen height when `inject_add_input_sources` is enabled.

inject_aux_display_id.description: Display ID to use for injected auxiliary display services.

inject_aux_width_margin.description: Auxiliary video width margin for injected display service.

inject_aux_height_margin.description: Auxiliary video height margin for injected display service.

inject_aux_density.description: Auxiliary display density (DPI) for injected display service.

inject_aux_viewing_distance.description: Auxiliary viewing distance for injected display service.

inject_aux_touch_width.description: Auxiliary input touchscreen width when `inject_add_input_sources` is enabled.

inject_aux_touch_height.description: Auxiliary input touchscreen height when `inject_add_input_sources` is enabled.

stop_on_disconnect.description: Stop automatic reconnection after manually selecting `Disconnect` in Android Auto on the phone. aa-proxy will not attempt to reconnect until the next restart or until a Bluetooth connection is initiated manually from the phone.

waze_lht_workaround.description: Enable a Waze workaround for LHT (Left-Hand Traffic) countries by swapping U_TURN_LEFT with U_TURN_RIGHT in navigation and HUD.

odometer.description: Enable odometer sensor reporting (for head units that don't provide this data). Once active, the current reading can be pushed via POST /odometer (value in km).

collect_speed.description: Enable speed collection from speed sensor for web socket, this value disables `remove_tap_restriction` by design.

hu_button_handler.description:
Path to a script or executable invoked on HU media-key long press.
Two arguments are always appended by aa-proxy-rs:
  1. keycode (u32) — Android key code of the long-pressed key
  2. elapsed_ms — how long the key was held, in milliseconds
Additional arguments embedded in the path are supported (shell-word splitting).
When empty or absent, HU media-key interception is fully disabled.
Requires `mitm = true`.

tire_pressure.description: Enable tire pressure sensor reporting (for head units that don't provide this data). Once active, readings for up to 4 tires can be pushed via POST /tire-pressure (values in kPa, order: FL, FR, RL, RR).

ev_battery_logger.description:
Path to executable for providing EV battery data (e.g. from an OBD dongle). It is started automatically when an Android Auto session begins and terminated when the session ends.
Any parameters provided at the end of the path are passed to the executable as-is.

ev_connector_types.description: List of EV connector types supported by your car. Select one or more values. If left empty, the default is EV_CONNECTOR_TYPE_MENNEKES.

dhu.description: Use a Google Android Auto Desktop Head Unit emulator instead of real HU device (will listen on TCP 5277 port). For usage without a real SBC!

aa_server_tcp_addr.description: Optional direct TCP address for Android Auto Head Unit Server on the phone/MD side, for example 127.0.0.1:5278 or 192.168.1.9:5279. Leave empty to keep the normal USB/Bluetooth/Wi-Fi MD transport. When set, aa-proxy-rs skips the Bluetooth/Wi-Fi AA handshake and opens this TCP connection only after the HU/DHU side is ready. Also don't forget to run `socat TCP-LISTEN:5279,bind=0.0.0.0,reuseaddr,fork TCP:127.0.0.1:5278`

eth_mode.description:
Configure Ethernet mode (optional). Options:
- `DHCP` (case-insensitive): dynamic IP assignment
- Static IP: e.g. `192.168.100.1/24`
- Leave blank to disable

usb_serial_console.description: Enables the USB serial gadget (ttyGS0) for low-level console debugging. This should be disabled during normal Android Auto operation.
